claim_gate.reason: The LLL embedding is a meaningful deterministic polynomial candidate, but finite recovery collapses in the tail and no inverse-polynomial coverage or reversible circuit theorem exists.

embedding_records.caveat: LLL need not return a particular short solution when many unrelated short vectors compete.
embedding_records.mathematical_role: a solution is represented by first coordinates 2x_i-1 in {-1,+1}

embedding_records.caveat: Polynomial bit complexity does not imply inverse-polynomial random-instance coverage.
embedding_records.mathematical_role: allows sum_i x_i a_i-t to vanish modulo N rather than over the integers

embedding_records.caveat: Scanning one or two reduced-basis vectors is a restricted polynomial extraction class.
embedding_records.mathematical_role: orients a reduced vector with target-row coefficient -1

embedding_records.caveat: Growing arity would reintroduce superpolynomial enumeration and is not promoted.
embedding_records.mathematical_role: searches cancellations missed by a single reduced basis row

falsifiers_triggered: High small-n LLL recovery near density one decays sharply and is not asymptotic evidence.
Polynomial LLL bit complexity alone does not prove inverse-polynomial random legal-input coverage.
Fixed-arity reduced-basis combinations remain polynomial but do not justify growing-arity enumeration.
Every returned witness is checked exactly; solver failure is not confused with target illegality at large n.
A source-contract claim still requires a uniform coverage theorem and reversible deterministic implementation.

solver_contract.algorithm: exact integer LLL on the modular target embedding, followed by fixed-arity reduced-basis scan
solver_contract.finite_evidence_rule: no finite success rate is marked source-contract satisfying without a scaling theorem
solver_contract.input: uniform A in Z_N^(n+O(1)) and uniform target t
solver_contract.output: one binary witness x with <A,x>=t mod N, or error
solver_contract.polynomial_resource_scope: dimension O(n), entry bit length O(n), fixed delta/scales/arity
solver_contract.promotion_requirement: uniform inverse-polynomial overall success implies inverse-polynomial legal-input coverage, plus a reversible implementation

summary: Ran 2016 deterministic LLL partial-solver trials across 252 rows through n=48. Finite success rows=131; tail success rows=1/72; source-contract rows=0.
